title: YouTube CEO Neal Mohan on AI, Censorship & the Future of Creators
description:
(0:00) Introducing YouTube CEO Neal Mohan

(1:08) YouTube's revenue split with creators

(7:30) How Neal thinks about enabling monoculture and connective threads at YouTube

(11:23) Censorship on YouTube, demonetization decisions, dealing with different regimes and laws globally

(19:58) YouTube TV, YouTube Premium, and channel subscriptions

(24:00) Generative AI on YouTube: labeling AI, likeness concerns, possibilities

analysis.summary: YouTube CEO Neal Mohan discusses AI, censorship, and the future of creators on the platform. He shares his thoughts on enabling monoculture and connective threads, as well as the challenges of dealing with different regimes and laws globally. The conversation also covers YouTube TV, YouTube Premium, and channel subscriptions.

takeaways.text: YouTube's revenue split with creators is a key aspect of the platform's business model
Neal Mohan thinks about enabling monoculture and connective threads at YouTube
Censorship on YouTube is a complex issue with varying laws and regimes globally
YouTube TV, YouTube Premium, and channel subscriptions offer alternative revenue streams
Generative AI on YouTube raises concerns about labeling and likeness
YouTube must balance free speech with regulation and safety
The platform's future depends on its ability to adapt to changing user behaviors and technological advancements

sentiment.notes: The conversation covers both positive and negative aspects of YouTube's current state and future prospects, reflecting a mixed sentiment.

notable_moments.description: Introduction of YouTube CEO Neal Mohan
